name: Progressive Link
preceding_figure_names: Back Corte, Basic Reverse Turn, Brush Tap, Chase, Closed Finish, Closed Promenade, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Four Step Change, Open Finish, Open Promenade, Open Reverse Turn, Lady In Line, Open Reverse Turn, Lady Outside, Outside Swivel, Progressive Side Step Reverse Turn, Promenade Link, Reverse Outside Swivel, Rock Turn, Walk
following_figure_names: Closed Promenade, Open Promenade, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Promenade Turn, Promenade Link, Back Open Promenade, Fallaway Promenade, Chase
notes:
The figure has solid, sharp feeling, not soft. It is more of an "upward" action with body projecting upward. 90 body rotation in body seems to give the right look and impact.

Promenade position:
Lady's L hip feels like rolled downward and backward, R hip fwd stretched, toward Man.
Hip is level, Lady may feel R hip is higher.
Hips has an diagonal angle to Man. L hip is "released". Upper body is closed more.
Actions are in hips, nothing should affect top shoulder line, arm relaxed, with tone, not noodly and don't change elbow during the step.
R hand: do not bend wrist back. wrist forward.
L hand: maintain "up and out" position, do not slide.
Head turning: should not be on its own, shouldn't be too late or too early. Turning the head when weight is changing onto LF.
Head change finishes when the action finishes.

The figure starts in DW. But often, link is preceded by 2 walks which started DW. Walks naturally curve to left, so the Link will end up with Man's feet facing LOD, Woman's feet facing center., traveling DC.

steps.action: LF forward in CBMP, DW, H, No Turn, No CBM; turn frame when weight on left foot, then put down right foot under shoulder in promenade position; create torque to wind up lady before turning her; turn the lady to Right, not Left; man steps beneath the lady.

RF side to side and slightly back in PP, Facing DW, IE of foot and IE of B of LF, 1/4 to R, No CBM; release tension of CBM, RF steps under right shoulder, frame turns sharply right; rotate thigh and hips first, then upper torso catches up, elbow never goes behind body; lady is in front Man, but behind right hip; frame turning to R at same time to lead lady into a link; make it quick and short; head should always be at the same level; avoid lowering on a walk.
comments: Rotate thigh and hips first, then upper torso catches up; elbow never goes behind body.
Do not raise right shoulder; keep big frame.

steps.action: LF side, slightly back in PP, Facing DC, IE BH of LF and IE of B of RF, 1/4 to R; position slightly back after 1/4 turn, not LOD; body more or less facing wall, Hip FDC; stay on Man's R, stepping a bigger step, to end behind Man in promenade position; swivel on Ball of LF, hip turn 1/4 to R, but body turns 1/8; keep body as still as possible; rib and hip rotate 1/8 relative to each other; knees together, not open; L hip higher; extend from 7th vertebra bend back; connection with Man moves lower to hip/leg; do not turn away from Man; right shoulder stays with Man; try to get shoulder to square with Man; do not turn shoulder out; R hand wrist forward; L hand maintain \"up and out\" position; head turning finishes when action finishes; figure has solid, sharp feeling, not soft; 90 body rotation in body seems to give the right look and impact.
comments: Rib and hip should rotate 1/8 relative to each other.
L hip is higher (but not falling forward).
Do not turn away from Man; keep shoulder square.
Do not change elbow during the step.
Head turning should finish when the action finishes.

steps.action: RF back in CBMP, BDW, BH, No Turn, No CBM; start winding up to left (rotate L hip to L, R hip fwd); RF strong CBMP (underneath body, crossing in the back more to give Man room); think about front of lower R thigh against back lower L thigh tightly; RF follows where center moves; R toe in, outside edge first, small step; after putting Ball of RF down, R heel will kiss the floor before transfer weight to LF on next step; stay very left behind in Man's R arm throughout; hip is quite closed up to Man; center is to Man; to increase impact, turn left first on this step and then sharp turn to R on next step; it's not just placement of the foot, but also shift of the center; PP: pick up R side, project to Man.

LF side, slightly back in PP, Facing DC, IE BH of LF and IE of B of RF, 1/4 to R; position slightly back after 1/4 turn, not LOD; body more or less facing wall, Hip FDC; depending on direction Man wants to go, this could be almost in place or side/slightly back; stay on Man's R, stepping a bigger step, to end behind Man in promenade position; swivel on Ball of LF, hip turn 1/4 to R, but body turns 1/8; keep body as still as possible; rib and hip rotate 1/8 relative to each other; knees together, not open; L hip higher; extend from 7th vertebra bend back; connection with Man moves lower to hip/leg; do not turn away from Man; right shoulder stays with Man; try to get shoulder to square with Man; do not turn shoulder out; R hand wrist forward; L hand maintain \"up and out\" position; head turning finishes when action finishes; figure has solid, sharp feeling, not soft; 90 body rotation in body seems to give the right look and impact.
comments: Rib and hip should rotate 1/8 relative to each other.
L hip is higher (but not falling forward).
Do not turn away from Man; keep shoulder square.
Do not change elbow during the step.
Head turning should finish when the action finishes.
